output:
Let me read the current CSV first. TC-006 currently uses regex notation (`^https?://`) which is developer-centric. I'll rewrite it in plain, tester-friendly language. **TC-006 has been rewritten with clearer language.** Here is a summary of what changed:

| Field | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| **Summary** | `Learn more link href is a valid URL` | `Learn more link opens a valid web address` |
| **Description** | Technical regex jargon (`^https?://`) | Plain English — explains *why* the check matters (so clicking it opens a real webpage) |
| **Data** | `absolute URL starting with http:// or https://` | Added a concrete example: `https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved` |
| **Test Steps** | 4 steps using regex notation | 5 steps in plain language — "Confirm the value starts with 'http://' or 'https://'" |
| **Expected outcome** | `href ... matches ^https?://` | Full plain-English sentence explaining what the result means to a non-technical reader |

All other test cases (TC-001 – TC-005) are unchanged. `docs/actualtestcases.csv` is now updated. ✅
